2

Level 2

Secondary School

Interactive mockup

The Teenager Circle training protocol for Liceo Majorana students, formalised for Phase II (AY 2026/27). The three tabs below show how this year's cohorts — 3A, 4E and 5A — each play a distinct role in the same pipeline: from story, to comic, to live delivery and evaluation.

This is a navigation mockup over real cohort roles and outputs — it does not call a live AI system. Phase II will turn this into an actual delivery tool.

Class 3A — Storytellers

The cohort that originates the stories — characters, settings and themes drawn from the territory (Avola's agro-industrial chains, inclusion, ecology) that later become the raw material for the rest of the pipeline.

Training protocol modules

  1. Module 1 — Brief: territory, themes and the Talento·Valore·Successo arc as a writing constraint
  2. Module 2 — Drafting: small-group story creation with generative AI as a co-writing tool
  3. Module 3 — Review circle: peer feedback before handoff to 4E

Output

23 stories produced and catalogued (e.g. "Felix Mentegatti e l'inganno digitale", "Il Sogno di Thabo") — the dataset behind the public Storie section of palestra.ai.

Class 4E — Visual pipeline operators

The cohort that turns 3A's stories into AI-generated comic strips — and prepares the "wrong" outputs that Station 4 (Comic-Strip Review) uses with primary pupils.

Training protocol modules

  1. Module 1 — Prompting craft: from story text to visual prompts, panel by panel
  2. Module 2 — Generation & triage: running the pipeline, selecting outputs (including imperfect ones, deliberately)
  3. Module 3 — Annotation: tagging errors by category (missing detail, wrong detail, stereotype, inconsistency) for Station 4 materials

Output

Comic strips + an error-annotation set that becomes the Comic-Strip Review material for primary pupils.

Class 5A — AI ambassadors / interviewers

The cohort that runs the Four Stations live with primary pupils, then conducts the POST 1-to-1 interviews — the same students are both educators and the measurement instrument.

Training protocol modules

  1. Module 1 — Facilitation training: station scripts, pairing, classroom management
  2. Module 2 — Live delivery: rotating through the Four Stations with primary classes
  3. Module 3 — POST evaluation: 1-to-1 interviews using the Vademecum Liceale Intervistatore

Output

Live delivery of the 19 May 2026 event + the POST evaluation dataset (71% / 72% / 78% / 64% station ratings).

From PCTO to "AI ambassador"

A formalised role, not an assignment

Each cohort receives a preparation phase before stepping into a primary classroom — a defined role, a station-based curriculum, and an explicit accountability framework. The 5A interviewer protocol (the Vademecum Liceale Intervistatore) is a real artefact from the May 2026 POST evaluation.

Read the Teenager Circle methodology

Phase II — AY 2026/27 (roadmap)

  • • Generalised across additional Liceo cohorts beyond 3A/4E/5A
  • • Dedicated measurement architecture for the Liceali themselves (not only primary pupils)
  • • Project-review tooling for tutors/teachers (this app)
See Level 3 — University & Executive Ed →
AI Palestra.AI Platform

Educational architecture for human–AI co-formation. Jointly developed with the schools and partners of the Palestra AI pilot.
